{{Quote|His housing of agents at his home in Shad on Melinz, though a huge risk, has saved many deep undercover agents from capture.|Personal addendum by General Airen Cracken to an entry of a datafile detailing Rebel Alliance operatives dedicated to Ytavarg Aleema|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Melinz''' was a terrestrial astronomical object<ref name="CRO" /> located in the [[Melinz system/Legends|Melinz system]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> It featured foothills situated within the limits of the [[City/Legends|city]] [[Shad (city)/Legends|Shad]].<ref name="CRO" />

At some point during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Ger Plortor]], a scout for the prominent [[Shockball/Legends|shockball]] team [[Shad Furies/Legends|Shad Furies]], visited the<ref name="CRO" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="EAOC Keedad">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Keedad system/Legends|Keedad system]]</ref> [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Keedad]] while on a vacation. There, Plortor met the [[Keed]] [[Ytavarg Aleema]], whom the scout recruited into joining their team. Accompanying Plortor back to Shad, Aleema became a member of the Shad Furies and, eventually, the most famous shockball player in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he also became an [[Foster Agent|agent]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], using his Shad [[Ytavarg Aleema's mansion|mansion]] to temporarily house other Rebel agents in need of shelter. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] that shortly followed the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] mentioned Melinz in an entry of a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives dedicated to Aleema.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Melinz was created by [[Craig Robert Carey]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Melinz [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square L-9.<ref name="TEA" />
